publishedAbstract: There may be instances, such as an OSD migration to a new datastore/storageclass with PVC-backed rook-ceph-mon resources. For OSD migrations to a new datastore/storageclass where the old storageclass/datastore will need to be removed, this is a mandatory step. Not doing so, and removing the old storageclass while the mons are still backed by that storageclass can cause data loss. Additionally, there may be inconsistencies between the rook-ceph-mon placement (scheduled nodes) and the rook-ceph-mon-endpoints configmap, where a redeployment of the rook-ceph-mon resource is needed. Accomplishing the redeployment is safe and easy as long as the warning is adhered to, as this process will only scale the rook-ceph-mon resource down for enough time for Rook to reconcile the discrepancy.
publishedTitle: How to Redeploy rook-ceph-mon Resources in OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)
